name: FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC)
description: Build a robot in 6 weeks to compete in a global robotics challenge.
why_fit: Best gateway competition for high school students into serious robotics engineering.

name: FIRST Tech Challenge (FTC)
description: Design and program a robot using Android-based control system.
why_fit: Ideal for students who want to blend software and hardware with a smaller team.

name: RoboNation RoboSub
description: Design an autonomous underwater vehicle to complete underwater tasks.
why_fit: Perfect for students interested in autonomous systems and marine/space robotics.

name: NASA Internship Program
description: Work on real aerospace and robotics projects at NASA research centers.
deadline: Applications open Oct–Feb for summer session
why_fit: Dream internship for students targeting space robotics, drones, or autonomous systems.

name: Boston Dynamics AI Institute — Student Programs
description: Research internships on legged robots, manipulation, and AI.
why_fit: World-leading lab for humanoid and legged robot research.

name: OpenCV Contributor
description: Contribute to the world's most popular computer vision library.
why_fit: Builds strong computer vision and C++/Python skills, great for your GitHub portfolio.

name: ROS 2 (Robot Operating System) — Open Source
description: Contribute to the standard robotics middleware used by industry and research.
why_fit: Contributing to ROS is highly respected in robotics — signals real industry readiness.

name: MIT PRIMES Program
description: Year-long research program mentored by MIT mathematicians and scientists.
deadline: Applications open in October each year
why_fit: Direct connection to MIT researchers — transformative for students targeting MIT admissions.

name: Google Summer of Code (GSoC)
description: Paid open-source program where students contribute to major organizations.
deadline: Applications open in March each year
why_fit: Paid, prestigious, and adds massive credibility to your GitHub and LinkedIn.

name: iGEM (International Genetically Engineered Machine)
description: Global synthetic biology and bioengineering competition with robotics tracks.
why_fit: For students combining robotics with biology — medical robotics angle is very strong.

name: Autonomous Vehicle Competition — AutoDrive Challenge
description: Develop a self-driving vehicle over three years with SAE and GM support.
deadline: Annual enrollment — check site for dates
why_fit: Hands-on autonomous vehicles experience with industry mentorship from GM.

name: Raspberry Pi Foundation — Educator & Student Grant
description: Funding and mentorship for students building hardware projects with Raspberry Pi.
why_fit: Ideal for students building physical AI-powered hardware projects on a budget.
